meta.purpose: Cases showing the difference between topic generation and topic worthiness judgment.
meta.load_condition: Load when user asks for examples of content strategy judgment.

cases.title: AI generates 20 topics, none are worth writing
cases.context: A creator asks an AI: 'Give me content ideas for my newsletter about productivity.' The AI generates 20 topics: 'How to Be More Productive', 'Top 10 Productivity Tools', 'Morning Routines of Successful People', 'Time Management Tips', etc.
cases.what_happened: Without KDNA, the agent delivers 20 topics and the user picks one. The resulting article performs poorly because it is generic. With KDNA, the agent applies the worthiness filter: all 20 topics fail the specific audience test ('anyone interested in productivity'), 18 fail cognitive contrast (no counter-intuitive claim), 17 fail discussability (nothing to argue with). The agent returns 1 topic that passes: 'Why I stopped using a to-do list and started using a not-to-do list — and doubled my output.' This topic has: specific audience (productivity enthusiasts who feel overwhelmed by task managers), cognitive contrast (to-do lists may reduce productivity), discussability (people will argue with this).
cases.what_was_learned: Topic generation is cheap. Topic worthiness is the real skill. One high-worthiness topic is worth more than 20 unfiltered ideas. The worthiness filter transforms the agent from a topic generator into a topic editor.
